promise.line2: — from two years of living in South Africa, with a child, with no plan B.

worlds.title: Ten deep dives — one for every topic that decides your emigration
worlds.subtitle: Pillar articles with everything we know on each topic. Extensive, personal, with concrete numbers and all sub-articles on one page.
worlds.more_coming: All topic worlds are live — ten thematic ones plus a personal category. Each pillar page bundles the essentials plus all blog articles on the topic.

pillars.visa: Nine visa types compared — including the Financial Independence Permit. Which fits which situation, what it costs, how long it takes.
pillars.job: Locally employed, remote for a German employer, or self-employed? Honest salary ranges, the tax reality, and which structure fits which situation.
pillars.auto: Which vehicle, new or used, insurance, and converting your German license. With real prices and our own setup choice.
pillars.immobilien: Rent or buy? Regions, prices, and the honest answer why we still haven't bought after two years — and why that's often the right move.
pillars.anschluesse: Internet, power, SIM, loadshedding — the first 14 days in South Africa and the backup setup you actually need.
pillars.sicherheit: The most honest answer to "Is South Africa safe?" — reality check, regional differences, our nine routines, and what it actually costs per month.
pillars.steuern: Exit tax, double taxation treaty, SARS registration. Without lawyer jargon, but with clear decision points and our own case.
pillars.krankenversicherung: Choosing Medical Aid: Discovery, Momentum & co., waiting periods, late joiner penalty, and why gap cover is mandatory.
pillars.umzug: Container options, customs, timing, picking the right shipper. Plus the uncomfortable truth: a lot of your German furniture doesn't belong in the container.
pillars.banking: Wise, Capitec, FNB: the three-account structure for expats, transfer strategies, and the typical mistakes with German home banks.
pillars.allgemein: Our most personal articles — origin story, biggest mistakes, DE-vs-SA life comparisons. Not theory, but lived experience.

topics.title: Everything you need to know
topics.subtitle: From the first visa question to your child's first day of school. Ten topic areas we've walked through ourselves.

personal.title: Two parents, one child, one decision.
personal.p1: In 2024 we packed up in Hamburg, sent a container to Cape Town, and boarded a plane with our child. No plan B. With far too many open questions.
personal.p2: Two years later, South Africa is home. On this site we share everything we've learned — including the mistakes that will hopefully save you your nerves and a few thousand euros.

hero.title: We're Laura & Finn
hero.subtitle: A German family who moved to South Africa — with our child, without a plan B. Today we help others walk the same path.

story.heading: How it started
story.p1: It started with a three-week holiday in Cape Town — and the realisation that we didn't want to go back. Two years later we were sitting in a half-furnished Airbnb in Bloubergstrand with three suitcases, our child, and a container somewhere on the high seas.
story.p2: At that point we had done a lot of research, but had even more open questions. Most online guides were either shallow, outdated, or written by consultants trying to sell emigration as a product — not as a life.
story.p3: This site is what we ourselves would have needed back then: honest, first-hand, no sales fluff. Including the mistakes we made. And there were quite a few.

mission.heading: Our mission
mission.p1: We want to be the first and most honest resource for German-speaking people moving to South Africa. Not theory — practical knowledge from everyday life. Not generic checklists — decision support for your specific situation.

v1.title: Honesty over reach
v1.body: We decline even when a product or provider doesn't deliver what it promises — regardless of how good the commission is.

v2.title: Experience over theory
v2.body: Every guide is based on what we've done ourselves or what families on the ground have lived through. No research-only articles without a practice test.

v3.title: Currency over completeness
v3.body: Laws, prices, visa rules change. We revise articles regularly and show openly when something was last verified.

v4.title: You decide
v4.body: We give you information, not ready-made answers. Every family is different, every emigration too. Ultimately you walk your own path.
